# SubNiche — Copilot Workspace Instructions

## Project stack

- **Framework**: Next.js 15.5 (App Router, React 19)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS 4 + CSS Custom Properties (OKLCH)
- **Language**: TypeScript 5 (strict mode)
- **UI components**: shadcn/ui (managed via `npx shadcn@latest add`)
- **Icons**: lucide-react
- **Database**: PostgreSQL via Supabase + Prisma v6
- **Validation**: Zod v4
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Deployment**: Vercel

---

## Before writing any E2E test

Read `.claude/agents/playwright.md` in full before writing anything that touches:
- `e2e/` — tests, page objects, fixtures, helpers
- `playwright.config.ts`

That file covers: Page Object Model pattern, auth fixture (JWT bypass), selector priority rules, what to test, test isolation rules, and the full coverage map. Do not write any test without reading it.

---

## Before writing any backend code

Read `.claude/agents/backend.md` in full before planning or writing anything that touches:
- `server/` — controllers, services, repositories, models, validators, mappers, middleware
- `app/api/` — route handlers
- `prisma/` — schema and migrations
- `lib/prisma.ts`, `lib/supabase/`, `lib/swagger/`

That file is the single source of truth for layer responsibilities, Prisma vs Supabase usage, error handling, Zod v4 conventions, OpenAPI registration, and naming rules. Do not proceed until you have read it.

---

## Before writing any frontend code

Read `.claude/agents/frontend.md` in full before planning or writing anything that touches:
- `components/`
- `app/` pages or layouts
- `app/globals.css`
- Any styling or Tailwind classes

That file is the single source of truth for component patterns, Tailwind usage, TypeScript conventions, accessibility standards, and naming rules. Do not proceed until you have read it.

---

## When importing v0.dev designs

When the user pastes code generated by v0.dev, or asks to integrate a v0 design:

1. Read `.claude/agents/v0-import.md` in full before touching any code
2. Follow every step in that file in order — do not skip the audit or the final checklist
3. Key things that always need attention on v0 code:
   - Install any missing shadcn components via CLI (`npx shadcn@latest add <name>`), never copy manually
   - Check Tailwind CSS 4 compatibility (v0 defaults to Tailwind CSS 3 syntax)
   - Remove unnecessary `"use client"` directives — only keep where interactivity actually exists
   - Extract hardcoded data to props
   - Convert default exports to named exports

---

## Backend boundaries — hands off

Do NOT modify files in these paths:

- `server/` — controllers, validators, services, decorators
- `app/api/` — Next.js route handlers
- `lib/swagger/` — OpenAPI spec
- `prisma/` — schema and migrations
- `lib/prisma.ts`, `lib/supabase/` — data clients

If a frontend task requires a backend change, output this block and continue with whatever frontend work is possible:

```
BACKEND CHANGE NEEDED
Requested by: frontend task — [brief description]
File to change: [exact file path]
What's needed: [describe the change]
Why: [what the frontend needs and why current backend doesn't support it]
```

---

## Code standards (summary)

Full details are in `.claude/agents/frontend.md`. The non-negotiables:

- **No `any` types** — always use explicit TypeScript interfaces
- **Named exports only** — no `export default` for components
- **kebab-case filenames** — `item-card.tsx`, not `ItemCard.tsx`
- **No hardcoded colors** — use CSS variable tokens (`text-foreground`, `bg-primary`, `bg-brand`)
- **No inline styles** — Tailwind only, unless there is no Tailwind equivalent
- **Accessibility attributes** — `aria-*`, `role="alert"`, `aria-hidden="true"` on decorative icons
- **`"use client"` only when needed** — components with `useState`, event handlers, browser APIs
- **`cn()` is available** at `@/lib/utils` — use it for UI primitives in `components/ui/`
- **`array.join(" ")` pattern** for feature components in `components/{feature}/`

---

## Folder conventions

```
components/ui/          ← shadcn-managed primitives (CLI only, never edit directly)
components/{feature}/   ← domain-specific components (items, auth, landing, etc.)
app/{route}/page.tsx    ← Next.js pages
app/globals.css         ← theme variables (OKLCH, light + dark)
lib/utils.ts            ← cn() helper
```

---

## API response shape

All backend responses use `{ data: T | null, error: string | null }`. Always destructure accordingly:

```tsx
const { data, error } = await res.json()
if (!res.ok) {
  setError(error ?? "Something went wrong")
  return
}
```

---

## Auth token

JWT is stored in `localStorage` under the key `"auth_token"`.
